task: Write a Python function longest_word(s) returning the longest whitespace-separated word in the string. On ties return the last such word, not the first. An empty string returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code correctly handles an empty string as input.
trace: To address this task, I decided to split the input string into individual words using `split()`, which handles consecutive whitespace by creating empty strings that I filter out with a condition. I then find the maximum length of any word in the list. To handle ties and return the last longest word as specified, I iterate over the words in reverse order and return the first one whose length matches the maximum length found. This approach ensures that if there are multiple words with the same maximum length, the last one encountered is returned, adhering to the task requirements.
